item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept and how the article, especially the logs, are written, but the story itself doesn't do much for me. A lot of things that happen to them seem very random, and if there is a logic behind that imagery, I didn't see it. On the other hand, the imagery itself isn't scary or surreal enough for me, so I didn't feel a lot while reading the logs. I don't think the article is bad, but I was a little underwhelmed when I finished reading it. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only issue is:

Subject D can be heard screaming wetly

The phrase "screaming wetly" doesn't sound especially clinical, and something about it just sounds jarring to me. I'd drop "wetly" or change it to something like "Subject D can be heard making gurgled screams".

But aside from that, I love all of the surreal imagery here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The found footage stuff is good and there's a solid idea here, but the details loose me.

I think one of my big issues is that at it's core, it's not an actual skip. The only thing which makes 3517 itself anomalous is that the contents change subtly between viewings, but that doesn't really connect to the meat of the article.
At it's core 3517 is not an actual anomaly, it's footage of an anomalous event. The changing contents technically push it over that boundary, but that's all they do and it feels like that's their only reason for being here.

If 3517 was the mountain itself - the actual anomaly - and the video log was a supporting document, then I feel this could be really solid. Unfortunately, as it stands -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“517” and A and B

Did I understand this correctly…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the data considered nonanomalus? It clearly is, among other things how it changes between viewings is not something that sort of data is not supposed to do

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is, actually: weeping wounds are ones that seep pus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject C screams in terror as she is coated with material, before collapsing and seizing, skin beginning to weep as black whistling growths burst from soft tissue.

"skin beginning to weep" isn't very clinical is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP. For me the scant detailing adds to its appeal; I like the kind of unfinished aspect of it. The whole thing feels weirdly real to me, kind of a like a recurring nightmare (with the subtly changing flora/fauna on each playing, the mountain always staying the same height, the slow change from normal to surreal). There's not much there, but there's an implication of something more.

I agree that the characters don't really draw me in and I don't really get much of a sense of A and B's closeness etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say this one grabbed me, overall. The shifting format of the video logs was jarring and made them a little more tedious to follow, and I feel like further logs with more examples of anomalous animals and activity, and maybe less focus on slight changes to topography, could really help. No vote for now, but I'm ready to upvote if it engages me more, after some edits.

Also, not sure if anyone else pointed this out (I didn't see it mentioned) but;

The contents of SCP-3517 change subtly between viewings, most often the location of environmental details such as clouds and distant trees, but retain most events and dialogue between viewings; beyond this, SCP-3517 is not otherwise considered anomalous in itself.

This wording doesn't make much sense. It reads like "The car is blue, except for interior detailing and outside trim; beyond this, the car is not otherwise considered blue".

I understand the use of the word "otherwise" clarifies the sentence, but it's confusing and sounds contradictory. A better wording would be something like;

beyond this, SCP-3517 has not demonstrated any additional anomalous properties

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had mixed feeling with this. Initially, I'm feeling intrigued by something that could potentially be creepy and weird, but it's not…. really that much.

No vote for now. Might change my mind later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mind reading unfinished work. This is disturbing enough and I love the atmosphere. I am looking forward to reading your new writing! Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Guns aren't some magical exception to not using US Customary measurements.

I feel like they are, but mind you, I'm no expert on guns nor formal writing.

I wonder if changing this is an allowable edit or not.

I feel like that would be crossing a line, but I'm also not staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Carries .22 rifle

Still should be 5.6mm (and probably chambered in 5.6×15mmR). Guns aren't some magical exception to not using US Customary measurements. I wonder if changing this is an allowable edit or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novote. This is some good found-footage, but as Cyan notes earlier, it seems superficial with not much else going on (or even, the notion that something is); at least, nothing I picked up on. The final action in the log didn't really go above and beyond what had came before, so the finale kinda flopped - it was just another thing that happens.

The pacing is really solid outside the final log, but lumping all the death and action back-to-back at the end, and killing everyone off made it feel rushed and unsatisfying. I think right after C dies is a good place to open this up for more story, as opposed to (or addition to) an added log. If you push that final action back, I feel like the piece would be stronger for it.

As a nitpick, I'd alter the first 'closing statement'. It's good, and eerie, but it's something that was already stated earlier in the log, so it didn't pack the punch it could have.

I'm looking forward to that update.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just downvote if you cant upvote

Nah, I'd only downvote it if I actually didn't like it, currently I neither like it nor dislike it, so I can't really do either. Don't wanna push it either way. What would be nice is if I could actually give a neutral vote, and it displays how many of those there are along with the total.

Thanks for the input!

You're welcome. Hope it was useful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just downvote if you cant upvote, its better that way. No offense taken. Thanks for the input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think I really… no sorry. This isn't bad in any way, it's just not enough for me to upvote. As has been said before, there's just not enough new stuff here. I really like the recorded/exploration type dealies where scary stuff happens and everyone dies, but this time I just don't think there's enough. Stuff like 1730 and 455 go on for a while, with good build-up, and 1981 is very creepy head on. This is kinda neither-here-nor-there with those, not enough build, and not enough direct action/events.

I don't feel connected with the characters in any way really, Subject A and B are supposed to have a close relationship, but I didn't see it other than B was very sad about his death. But so they would they all, surely. It also doesn't help A dies before we get to see any of him.

As for the nasty stuff on the mountain, well they're pretty scary, and you describe them well, especially with the bird, but I don't feel any connection. They're just a bunch of random, terrifying, creatures on a mountain, I don't see any links between them at all, no feature that connects all of them in some subtle way, that makes you think there's something bigger going on.

I guess that's it really, there's nothing bigger here. The best parts for me about 455, 1730, and 1981, was that you got the impression there was something behind it all, perhaps not someone, but at least something causing it. Here, it's just a creepy collection of stuff for some poor, unlucky, souls. At the very least some more happenings could be added to the logs.

There's meat on the bones, but no sinew, if you catch my drift. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this reminded me of Video Oddity a great deal as well. There is some great imagery, but this played out exactly as I imagined it would when I learned that it was going to be "found footage of doomed hikers."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't like this. It doesn't bring anything unique to the table, and there are unnecessary details such as the clothing material being denim, or the angle that the bird flew. Maybe it could be a regular skip but I don't find this fit to be the grand SCP 3000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is weird and creepy, but definitely feels as unfinished as the author post suggests. Also there's the already noted US Customary units (and somewhat under-powered firearm, but people do use target rifles for squirreling and similar sometimes, so not implausibly so). So no-vote for now, but looking forward to post-contest improvements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this enough to upvote it, but it really does feel like an early, somewhat undeveloped draft. What's here is very nice, but I want more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it does not win (which is probably impossible, as Anantashesha has at least 5 times as many upvotes), I will reassign it to a specific 3000+ number that reflects one of my previous SCPs' numbers.

I'll then update and boost up the logs, possibly completely rewriting large sections to better broadcast the ambiance I want to portray. This will be horror, but the horror of being lost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a combination of found footage movies and SCP-1981 Ronald Regan cut up while talking, which was disturbing. a little cliched, but is a nice read. wounder if the two are connected.

+1
Edit: If this doesn't win and become SCP-3000, what will you do with this SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Backup copies are kept in redundant file formats and multiple hard drives as per standard procedure regarding non-anomalous recorded media.

The contents of SCP-3000 change subtly between viewings

That is the main feature of anomalous media. I'm fine with having the video shift slightly just because, but the containment procedures need to match.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm. Approach me sometime about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dexanote's at it again!

Some parts of this skip really interest me, but there are some parts that come across flat. More specifically-
- The story's pacing in the logs was interesting but seemed to lack buildup. I'm not saying that you have to expand the first recording, but it would be nice to get a little more personality from the characters and make us feel a bit more attached to subject A before his demise.
- Ending a skip in the logs is something that works for some stories, but without further wrap-up, it lacks a cohesive ending and just feels cut off to me.
- Found footage/diary stories are a fairly established common format for creepypasta at this point. I feel it needs something more to set it apart. Does the Foundation locate the mountain in question? What's done about it? Or is the mountain itself not identifiable on any map?

What I liked a lot-
- The implication that each time you watch the footage, small details change. I'd honestly like to see more done with that.
- The deer splitting in two was a really interesting visual as well as the pheasant exploding into baby birds. The wildlife body horror going on is quality. Someone else said that the fox-vulture was a bit much, but honestly at that point the anomalous parts of the mountain were in full force, so it's a good time to not hold back.

I know you were rushed for time, and honestly could feel that while reading it. I look forward to seeing any further edits and fleshing-out. No vote right now, but I'll be keeping an eye out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Few edits, you can write " recordings are edited for brevity" so you don't have to explain so much about them, also wrong word usage, in following sentence

same height as it did in earlier shots, despite apparent constant decent.

Should be descent.

Okay sorry not really creepy or scary and not SCP format. More like a movie script vibe .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm. I like this a lot! It reminds me of the SAR stories in all the right ways. I've always been a sucker for forestry-based horror, and I'm actually working on building up some organizations and a canon which this might fit nicely into, if you're at all interested…

item.title: Time style inconsistencies
item.content:encoded: The story is solidly written and in a believable style but the structure of your time formatting is extremely distracting. It is okay to use the format 00:22:03 to indicate 22 minutes and 3 seconds. Some parts have the format in mm:ss and other in hh:mm:ss, it would be much better to be formatted consistently to feel more cohesive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some of the material here is interesting (the pheasant stands out as my favourite), but I just don't think it does enough to distinguish itself from A Video Oddity. It seems like that might change after the contest though, so I'll leave off voting for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh. What happens when you mix the Blair Witch Project with spooky animals and video oddity creepiness? You get Dex's 3000Con entry is what.

In all seriousness, I liked reading through the logs. The descriptions there are vivid and really give this article a nice chilling atmosphere. I actually wish that there was more here, because I felt that things could have been drawn out a little more before the various hikers met their fates. Still, what is here is neat and very much worth an upvote.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 23:41 is a typo, actually. On my Google Docs draft it's highlighted green for me to go back and update, but I hadn't before posting because I was running short on time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fauna and flora is better. I only recalled the fungus image partway through the writing process, missed that.

And RISE YES THAT WAS THE WORD I COULDN'T GRASP. Thank you Khym.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The contents of SCP-3000 show disturbing and surreal fauna populating the mountain,

Perishes after encounter with flora.

Should that be "surreal fauna and flora" (emphasis added), or "perishes after encounter with fauna"?

appears to loom

"Loom" is rather non-clinical. "Rise" would be an appropriate verb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once I got to the description, I knew I was gonna be in for a ride, and I sure was. :D

vanishing in a shower of viscera and dozens of what appear to be skinless chicks, which appear to squeal extremely loudly in agony as they land

I mean, geez! If you add more to this, I can't wait to see what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is 23:41 supposed to be hours and minutes or minutes and seconds?

As for the article, no vote. Some of the imagery was interesting, some of it (the fox in particular) came off pretty silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Caliber inches aren't SI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed reading it, but this doesn't really give me that creepy feeling like some of the other articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only qualms with this are the following:

Subject C aims and fires. Creature is struck in neck region, and turns to flee up the mountainside, apparently unharmed.

Subject C: I know I hit it too, I saw the hole. But it just fucking ran away.

It ran away unhurt because it's a large animal and the rifle is .22 caliber. People do hunt with .22 sometimes, but it's a tiny round and won't do shit to a large animal like this thing that attacked them. This would make more sense (since the character is a hunter, too) if it were .308 Winchester or .223 Remington (if you want to change it and want to avoid naming brands, just say .308 or 5.56). Edit: And what sirpudding said. I didn't even think of that.

My other, non-logistical issue with this is that it's really similar to this story, which I think is one of the fairly popular horror stories on the 'net. It's not a spitting image of it, for sure, and I'm alright reading content that feels old in a new format (the SCP format) but my mind making the connection still made this feel a little predictable. I don't think the issue is that it's similar to that story in particular, I think the issue is that this is creepy but doesn't have much else to it other than confusion and gore and fucked-up biology. We're not really given any hint as to why this is happening, we just see it happen, and the results aren't unique enough to stand on their own without origin context imo. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll be honest. This one doesn't get to me.
The procedures and description lack an intersting catch or twist to them. Anything that would hook me up.
The log itself seems rather tedious to read and frankly speaking is not scary to me.

Still, the potential is there and when the contest is over some editing might improve it a lot.
Some elaboration on geographical changes, maybe some images to break the monotony.

For now, no vote.

item.title: Re: Admin Post
item.content:encoded: Well thats attached to this page forever. CDi Zelda. Ty

item.title: Re: Admin Post
item.content:encoded: Do not respond to Admin posts if you are not a member of site administration.

item.title: Admin Post
item.content:encoded: This entry was posted several minutes after the contest posting period had technically ended. The entry was prepared prior to the end of the contest time limit, but was late due to formatting and technical issues. Therefore, it will not be disqualified from contention.

item.title: I DID IT. I CAN DO IT STILL.
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't manage what I wanted, but at least here I can sort of place.

Edit: i plan to post updates once the contest is over.

+ DOUBLE EDIT 
- Hide whatever

So to elaborate. I was struck with a great angle to approach the Hideous Mountain with the other day, and after schoolwork was finished I pushed myself to put this together as best I could. While I won't elaborate too deeply, it will ideally have a Series 1/Series 2 kind of feel and a running theme of something that I genuinely fear.

The barest undertones of that is in here, and I think I did pretty alright. But more will be added in the near future, after the contest. If it goes beyond adding to the transcript logs, I'll go to the Rewrite Team and get their input, and am willing to work from there.

This all comes after literal weeks of grappling with my first concept, a witch. But Fantem made a witch 3000 that at this time is doing fairly well, and for that I'm eternally thankful and in awe.

I'm just happy I could write something again.
